name: Midnight Scenes: Among Graves
short_description: "When the graves stop whispering in your ear, it’s time for you to surrender to eternal silence". So says the Agreement, and so it must be fulfilled. Welcome to a new dimension of horror. Welcome to Midnight Scenes.
detailed_description:
This game is a short interactive novel (60-90 minutes). Perfect to play right before bed!

In tonight's episode of Midnight Scenes, Matthew Turtle will get a visit that will change his life. His worst fear is about to materialize, and every moment could be his last.

In this dark tale about the fear of death and losing what you love the most, our protagonist will fight to change the course of his destiny. How far will he be willing to go?

Turn off the lights and enter the depths of Midnight Scenes: Among Graves.

This is the sixth in a series of short horror stories by Octavi Navarro (Thimbleweed Park, Photographs) and Susanna Granell, creators of the critically acclaimed games The Supper, The Librarian, and Unwelcome.

  • Enjoy this immersive horror tale through the eyes of Matthew, a man terrified by his imminent future.

  • Story-driven point-and-click experience that will take you on an unforgiving descent into the depths of hell.

  • Explore your surroundings, meet interesting characters...and stay alert!

  • Colorful pixel art inspired by horror and mystery classic shows like The Twilight Zone and The Outer Limits.

  • Eerie original soundtrack.

  • Atmospheric sound effects.

Can you avoid what’s inevitable?

reviews.review_text:
As much as I love Octavi's games for their immaculate atmosphere and art style, this one, unfortunately, was a miss for me.
The plot is a bit confusing and lacks this captivating mystery that Midnight Scenes are usually known for.
The gameplay is also entirely missing this time. It's more like a slideshow of a story rather than the usual interactable point & click.
Also, sound effects really throw off the atmosphere. Like, they're unnecessarily funny.
Overall, it might still be worth a try if you really like those games, but for me personally it didn't do it.

I had a nice time with this episode of Midnight Scenes but I wish there was a bit more point 'n click gameplay even if I know this is primarily a visual novel. I also still dislike the goofy sounds that come up during discussions (I even decided to mute them in the settings) because it breaks the dark atmosphere.

That being said that was a cool episode, but not the best nor the most thought provoking. My favorites ones are still The Nanny and A Safe Place. I'll be here for the next one though!

Midnight Scenes: Among Graves is a nice story to experience, but I think it could be better.

The characters mostly felt bland. I didn't feel any emotional connection with them (except for Carla during the end), but the story about the Veil and the lineage was intriguing.

I wish there were meaningful choices (if there were any, I missed whatever they changed in the story) and/or puzzles. Or minigames. I know this is a lineer interaction experience, rather than an adventure 'game', but I still wish it had a gameplay.

Still, it's a good story to experience.

Another solid atmospheric entry in this Twilight Zone-like series. They play like a point and click game but siimilar to some of the later games in the series, they don't have any inventory puzzles, and is pretty much on rails. It's the story that's important.
I really love this game and this franchise. A wonderful, heartwarming story and great atmosphere. The pixel-art is very atmospheric. I just wish it was a little bit longer.
